title: collomia cavanillesii f. coccinea
intro: Collomia cavanillesii f. coccinea, also known as the red-stemmed collomia, is a flowering plant species native to the western United States and Mexico. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The plant has been reported to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, and has been used to treat conditions such as fever, sore throats, and skin irritations. Collomia cavanillesii f. coccinea can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and has also been used as a component in topical creams and salves.
